data.title: Bayer Stock Gains on Proposed $7.25B Settlement of Roundup Cancer Litigation
data.description:
Bayer's (BAYRY) U.S.-listed shares rose 6% on Tuesday after the German pharma and agriculture giant announced a proposed $7.25 billion settlement to resolve lawsuits alleging that the company's Roundup weedkiller caused cancer. The company's Frankfurt-listed shares (DE:BAYN) gained 7.4% yesterday.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bayer has been caught up in the Roundup litigation since the completion of its $63 billion acquisition of Monsanto in 2018. The company has been facing allegations that glyphosate, the key ingredient in Roundup, causes cancer. However, Bayer has denied these accusations, saying that the Environmental Protection Agency and other regulators have determined that the product is safe to use.

More on Bayer's Proposed Roundup Settlement

Bayer disclosed that law firms representing tens of thousands of plaintiffs filed a motion on Tuesday seeking preliminary approval of the settlement in the Circuit Court of the City of St. Louis, Missouri. The settlement follows the Supreme Court's decision to hear Bayer's challenge to the Roundup litigation.

In January 2026, the Supreme Court agreed to hear an appeal from Bayer, which argued that consumers shouldn't be able to sue the company for failing to warn about cancer risks, because U.S. federal regulators had determined that glyphosate doesn't require a cancer warning. The court will hear arguments in the case in Spring 2026, with a ruling expected by early July. If Bayer wins, it could result in the dismissal of thousands of cases against the company. 

Bayer CEO Bill Anderson said that the proposed settlement, along with the Supreme Court case, "provides an essential path out of the litigation uncertainty." Given the proposed settlement, Bayer expects its provisions and litigation liabilities to increase from 7.8 billion euros to 11.8 billion euros. Also, the company expects around 5 billion euros in litigation‑related payments in 2026. Consequently, Bayer expects to report negative free cash flow for the year.

The company will now announce its 2025 results and 2026 guidance on March 4 ‌to reflect the settlement in its financial statements.

Is Bayer a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Bayer stock based on one Buy and one Hold rating. The average BAYRY stock price target of $12.35 indicates 14.2% downside risk. BAYRY stock has rallied 155% over the past year.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Apple Stock Selloff 'Unwarranted' – Wedbush's Dan Ives Sticks to Street-High $350 PT
data.description:
Wedbush's top analyst Daniel Ives called Apple's (AAPL) recent stock selloff "unwarranted" and maintained his Street-high price target of $350, implying 32.6% upside potential. The longtime Apple bull dismissed concerns about the Siri AI delay, focusing on "Apple getting its AI strategy right." After an 8% five-day drop on delay news, AAPL rebounded 3.2% yesterday after Ives' upbeat note.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ives warned that the market is "significantly underestimating" the transformative potential of Apple's upcoming Siri AI platform, slated for 2026. With a massive installed base of 2.5 billion iOS devices and 1.5 billion iPhones, Ives projects $75–$100 per-share value from AI monetization in coming years.

Ives ranks #636 out of the 12,108 analysts tracked on TipRanks. He has a 53% success rate and an average return per rating of 13.40%.

Ives Says 'This Time Is Different'

Ives acknowledged that investors "have endured this soap opera over the last year with Cupertino's lack of AI strategy," especially as rivals like Google (GOOGL) and Microsoft (MSFT) raced ahead with generative AI tools. Even so, he views the current panic overdone, emphasizing that Apple's advanced features are still on track for a summer 2026 rollout.

He stressed that this year's scenario is "way different" from last year's missteps. He highlighted Apple's partnership with Google Gemini, the addition of top external AI talent, and the internal recognition that its overhauled Siri architecture ranks among the company's most pivotal tech launches ever.

Ives noted that developers and consumers are "waiting patiently" for the enhanced Siri's phased rollouts, dubbing it "Apple's answer to ChatGPT and Perplexity." He also anticipates an AI-powered subscription service launching by autumn.

Is Apple a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

On TipRanks, Apple stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 17 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Apple price target of $307.55 implies 16.6% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, AAPL shares have gained nearly 8%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 2/19/26?
data.description:

data.title: Paramount Skydance (PSKY) Stock Jumps Despite Warner Bros. Rejection
data.description:
Shares of Paramount Skydance (PSKY) jumped 4.9% yesterday, despite facing another rejection from Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) for its sweetened bid. WBD sent Paramount a letter stating that Netflix (NFLX) has allowed WBD to open a seven-day window for deal talks with PSKY to "fully and finally resolve this matter." WBD claims the Ellison camp verbally agreed to raise its bid to $31 or more if talks began, which WBD has now agreed to do.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Even so, WBD clarified that it does not view Paramount's offer as superior and remains committed to Netflix's deal. It remains to be seen if the David Ellison-run PSKY will boost its offer above $31 per share to acquire all of WBD, including its news and studios divisions.

Here's What Happened

On February 10, Ellison revised his bid to include a quarterly ticking fee, Netflix breakup fee, and debt refinancing costs, while keeping the $30 per share offer intact. The next day, a senior Paramount financial advisor told WBD's board that PSKY would pay $31 per share if talks opened, and that $31 was not its top offer.

Subsequently, Netflix waived some merger agreement terms, allowing WBD to negotiate with PSKY until February 23 and clarify their proposal. WBD expects talks to exceed $31 per share and has asked Paramount for its best and final offer.

WBD reiterated that its board does not think Paramount's proposal beats the Netflix merger. They still recommend and fully back the Netflix deal, with a shareholder vote set for March 20, 2026.

Meanwhile, Netflix expressed confidence in its deal's superior value and certainty, calling PSKY's moves a distraction for stockholders and the industry. Hence, Netflix gave WBD a short 7-day waiver to talk with PSKY and end this once and for all.

Paramount and Netflix are battling for Warner Bros. due to its premier film/TV studios and huge content library, highlighting the high stakes in today's fast-changing entertainment world. Paramount accepted the 7-day window but called WBD's board moves "unusual." It plans to push its tender offer, challenge the "inferior" Netflix merger, and nominate directors for WBD's next annual meeting.

Is PSKY Stock a Good Buy?

No, analysts remain cautious on Paramount Skydance's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, PSKY has a Moderate Sell consensus rating based on zero Buys, one Hold, and three Sell ratings. The average Paramount Skydance price target of $12.33 implies 13.9% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, PSKY shares have dropped 4.2%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Cathie Wood Pours Millions into Broadcom, AMD, Coinbase – Sells $13M Teradyne
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio changes on Tuesday, February 17, as shown in daily fund disclosures. Wood accumulated shares in semiconductor firms Broadcom (AVGO) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), showing greater confidence in the sector despite the ongoing AI-related fears.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Moreover, she boosted the fund's stake in cryptocurrency platform Coinbase Global (COIN), reflecting optimism despite Bitcoin's (BTC-USD) price slump.

On the sell side, Wood continued trimming exposure to test-equipment maker Teradyne (TER), vacation rental firm Airbnb (ABNB), and social media site Pinterest (PINS). She also sold a large position in biotech firm Twist Bioscience (TWST), whose shares hit a fresh 52-week high of $53.42 yesterday.

Let's briefly understand Wood's trades:

Broadcom – Wood purchased 24,205 shares for roughly $8.05 million. AVGO stock gained 2.3% yesterday, amid optimism around custom AI chip manufacturers.

AMD – Wood bought 30,139 AMD shares totaling $6.12 million. AMD fell 2% amid reports of possible delays in the manufacturing of its Instinct MI455X accelerators. Moreover, AMD slipped another 2.3% in extended trading following news of Nvidia (NVDA) and Meta's (META) expanded, multiyear AI infrastructure partnership.

Coinbase – Wood also added 41,453 shares of Coinbase for $6.88 million. COIN stock was up 1% despite Mizuho Securities cutting its price target from $280 to $170 (3.5% upside). The firm prefers rival Robinhood (HOOD) amid the ongoing "crypto winter" and falling Bitcoin prices.

Teradyne – Wood sold 43,586 TER shares totaling $13.32 million, marking the largest trade of the day. TER stock fell 2.9% yesterday, after a post-earnings rally on February 2.

Twist Bioscience – Wood dumped 119,374 shares of the biotech player for roughly $6.31 million, capitalizing on the 7.7% stock surge after announcing a licensing agreement with Invenra. Twist became Invenra's co-exclusive provider of the B-Body bispecific antibody platform, gaining all related revenue and 6% ownership.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool:

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on Twist Bioscience, AMD, and Broadcom stocks, with AMD offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why SanDisk Stock Dropped 6% on WDC Stake Sale News
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock dropped 5.7% on February 17 and fell another 2% in after-hours trading following news that former parent Western Digital (WDC) plans to exit its entire stake in the memory chipmaker. According to a regulatory filing, WDC is selling 7.51 million shares at $535 to $555 per share.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that SanDisk will not receive any proceeds from this secondary share offering. Instead, WDC will raise up to $3.09 billion from this stock sale. The offer price represents a discount of 9.4% to 6% to SNDK's closing price of $590.59 yesterday.

Why Is Western Digital Selling SNDK Stock?

Western Digital aims to reduce its debt by exchanging the SanDisk shares for debt held by affiliates of JPMorgan (JPM) and Bank of America (BAC). As of December 31, 2025, WDC had $2.23 billion in short-term debt and $2.43 billion in long-term debt.

WDC had announced plans to sell its remaining 7.5 million SanDisk shares before the one-year anniversary of their spinoff, which closed on Feb. 24, 2025. CFO Kris Sennesael announced this during WDC's January 29 quarterly earnings call.

SanDisk Stock Price Movement

The offering coincides with a global shortage of flash memory chips, fueled by massive AI spending. Flash memory technologies such as NAND (Not AND) and DRAM (dynamic random-access memory) are essential for data storage/processing in AI data centers, devices, and servers, driving current demand spikes.

SanDisk's products power computers and phones, but the tech industry's boom-and-bust cycles have kept its stock valuation modest. With DRAM chip spot prices surging since September, shares of SanDisk and peers are likely to remain high at least into 2026. SNDK stock has skyrocketed more than 1,152% over the past year.

Is SNDK a Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts remain divided about SanDisk's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, SNDK stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys and four Hold ratings. The average SanDisk price target of $637.33 implies 7.9% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AMD Stock Slides While Nvidia Gains: What's Driving the Divergence Today (2/18/26)?
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares fell more than 2% in after-hours trading on Wednesday, while Nvidia (NVDA) edged higher as Meta Platforms (META) deepened its AI partnership with Nvidia. AMD also faced separate pressure from reports of delays in its upcoming AI chips, which may give Nvidia an additional advantage.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta is expanding its AI infrastructure and plans to deploy large numbers of Nvidia chips across its data centers to train and run AI models. Because Meta is one of the world's largest buyers of AI hardware, the decision quickly caught investors' attention.

Why the Meta Deal Hurt AMD

Meta's choice matters because large cloud and internet companies typically standardize their systems around one main supplier. That can leave limited room for competitors. After the announcement, AMD shares fell as investors saw fewer chances for its chips to win business inside Meta's growing data center network.

AMD faced additional pressure from reports of manufacturing issues with its upcoming Instinct MI455X accelerators. The problems may delay production and limit shipments of its Helios rack systems this year, with larger volumes now expected in 2026.

Together, the Meta decision and the product concerns raised doubts about AMD's near-term ability to compete for major AI data center contracts.

Why This Helps Nvidia

For Nvidia, the development reinforces its position in the AI chip market.

Large data centers often build their software and networking around the first hardware installed. Once deployed at scale, switching becomes difficult and expensive. Meta's deeper adoption of Nvidia hardware therefore not only supports near-term sales but also strengthens longer-term demand.

Possible delays in AMD's competing products also add to that advantage. With limited high-performance alternatives available soon, Nvidia is likely to face less direct competition in top-tier AI GPUs in the near term.

Which AI Chip Stock Do Analysts Prefer?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to see how Wall Street currently views Nvidia and AMD.

Both stocks carry a "Strong Buy" consensus rating from analysts. However, AMD shows slightly higher upside based on the average price target, even though Nvidia remains the larger and more dominant company in the AI chip market.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Did Nvidia Sell Applied Digital (APLD) and Buy Intel? New 13F Filing Reveals Changes
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) disclosed changes to its investment holdings in its latest quarterly 13-F filing covering positions as of December 31, 2025. The report shows the company sold its stake in Applied Digital (APLD) while also disclosing a large stake in Intel (INTC) along with a few other infrastructure-related companies. The update points to a shift toward hardware and networking suppliers that support AI data centers rather than smaller AI-linked businesses.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Nvidia Sold

The SEC filing indicates Nvidia exited multiple previously disclosed investments tied to the AI ecosystem. In its earlier report, Nvidia had owned millions of shares of Applied Digital, but  its latest filing revealed it had exited its roughly $177 million stake in the digital infrastructure company. 

Following the release of the filing, APLD shares tumbled 7.5% in after-hours trading Wednesday. The development caught investors off guard, as Nvidia had previously been viewed as a strategic backer, and its decision to sell raised questions about the company's outlook.

In addition to Applied Digital, Nvidia sold stakes in Arm Holdings (ARM) and WeRide (WRD).

Nvidia Adds Intel, Nokia, and Synopsys

The filing revealed a large disclosed position in Intel. Nvidia reported owning about 214.8 million Intel shares, worth roughly $7.9 billion at quarter-end.

The portfolio also listed positions in Nokia (NOK) and chip-design software firm Synopsys (SNPS). Both companies are closely linked to the hardware behind AI systems — networking equipment and semiconductor development tools.

The changes suggest Nvidia is putting more focus on the basic infrastructure needed to run large AI data centers.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 379 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $260.38 indicates 40.77% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Software Stocks Are Plummeting… Are Their Forward Estimates Next?
data.description:
Artificial intelligence is shaking up the software industry. Indeed, lightning-fast advances from companies like OpenAI, Google (GOOGL), and Anthropic are leading to serious worries about how traditional software businesses will compete. As a result, former high-fliers such as Workday (WDAY) and Salesforce (CRM) are seeing sharp declines in their stock prices. Even so, many Wall Street analysts have been slow to adjust their earnings forecasts, which suggests that they may not fully appreciate the long-term disruption risk.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That gap becomes clear when looking at the numbers. According to Goldman Sachs (GS) strategist Ben Snider, software stocks tracked by the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) have fallen about 24% over the past three months. However, during that same period, two-year forward earnings estimates for the group have actually increased by 5%. Likewise, other industries seen as vulnerable to AI, including insurance brokers and advertising companies, have also received positive revisions to their 2026 earnings estimates.

As a result, some strategists believe that the gap will eventually close, and not in a good way. This is because historically, price action often reflects underlying risks before analysts adjust their forecasts. Software stocks that once traded at price-to-earnings multiples near 35 times are now below 20 times, according to Innovator Capital Management's Tim Urbanowicz, who spoke to Yahoo Finance. He also went on to say that this sell-off highlights the "dark side" of AI and serves as a warning that disruption could spread to other sectors as well.

Is IGV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on IGV stock based on 91 Buys, 22 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IGV price target of $128.86 per share implies 59.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
